name: Atropine
executiveSummary: Bradycardia. To reduce upper airway secretions and salivation. Ophthalmology: mydriatic and cycloplegic.
indications:
## Main indications

Bradycardia. To reduce upper airway secretions and salivation. Ophthalmology: mydriatic and cycloplegic.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Bradycardia: 1 mg IV every 3–5 min (max. 3 mg).

pediatrico.dose: 20 µg/kg/dose (minimum dose: 100 µg); may be repeated at 5-minute intervals up to a total maximum dose: children: 1 mg; adolescents: 2 mg Toxicology: IV 20 µg/kg
pediatrico.notes: Do not administer IM in neonates. Administer undiluted by IV route. Contraindicated in urinary tract obstruction and glaucoma. For intratracheal administration, dilute with normal saline in a total volume of 1-2 ml.

stability:
## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Mydriasis, dry mouth, cycloplegia, photophobia, bradycardia followed by tachycardia, palpitations, arrhythmias, urinary retention, confusion, hallucinations.
